mechanism_narrative: A negative 3-month change in FEDFUNDS signals easier monetary conditions that lower discount rates on equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if negative 3-month FEDFUNDS changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of triggered observations or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An upward crossing in UNRATE signals weakening labor income and rising recession risk, reducing expected corporate revenues and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 5.5% or rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE deterioration is followed by nonnegative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates that employment income and consumer spending are expanding together, lifting earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS and RSAFS both cross above their 3-month moving averages
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-RSAFS trigger does not outperform either single-series trigger over 20 trading days or if triggered returns are positive in less than 52% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A downward crossing in INDPRO signals production cuts and weaker goods-sector operating leverage, lowering near-term earnings revisions and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if downward INDPRO crossings are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the conditional mean return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 6-month trend in HOUST leads residential investment, durable-goods demand, and construction-linked earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 6-month HOUST trends fail to produce higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative HOUST trends in at least 60% of rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%, negative real policy rates support nominal revenue growth and liquidity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change crosses above 3% while FEDFUNDS < 1%
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL acceleration and low-FEDFUNDS interaction has a nonpositive average 5-day S&P 500 return or does not outperform high-inflation periods with FEDFUNDS above 1%.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above its 12-month average while CPIAUCSL inflation is elevated, expected policy tightening raises discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its 12-month moving average while CPIAUCSL year-over-year change is above 3%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered tightening-inflation episodes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if their mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent gains in GDPC1 and INDPRO indicate broad real activity acceleration across aggregate demand and production, raising earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with simultaneous positive GDPC1 and INDPRO growth do not deliver higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than periods with only one series growing, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.
